name: Herald
description: API infrastructure connecting commercial-insurance brokers to carriers for real-time quoting and binding, with an AI layer that extracts and normalizes submission data from carrier-specific formats.

disruptionMechanisms: API-first infrastructure replacing manual re-keying between brokers and carriers
Carrier-connectivity network effects
attributes.problemStatement: Commercial insurance placement between brokers and carriers is still largely manual, with re-keyed submission data and fragmented per-carrier quoting workflows.
attributes.productDescription: Herald offers a unified API that connects brokerages to 80-plus insurance products across 35-plus carrier partners spanning cyber, management liability, professional liability, BOP, general liability, and workers' compensation, with AI-driven extraction of submission data into a standardized transaction format.

founders.name: Matt Antoszyk
founders.background: Led Herald from its December 2021 seed round through its October 2024 Series A; public sources reviewed do not disclose detailed prior career history.

name: Straddle
description: Identity-to-payment API combining identity verification, open banking, and real-time bank-to-bank (ACH/RTP) payments for SaaS platforms, payment facilitators, and marketplaces.

disruptionMechanisms: Replacing card-network rails with bank-to-bank payment
Bundling identity verification with payment execution in one API
attributes.problemStatement: Bank-to-bank payments lack the identity and fraud confidence of card transactions, forcing platforms to stitch together separate KYC, open-banking, and ACH vendors.
attributes.productDescription: Straddle's Pay by Bank API combines identity verification (SSN/ID/liveness checks), pre-debit balance confirmation (Watchtower), and ACH/RTP payment execution with built-in AML and fraud monitoring, settling within 24 hours.

founders.name: Keith Raphael
founders.background: Based in Broomfield, Colorado; public sources reviewed do not disclose detailed prior career history.

name: Mercoa
description: Embedded B2B payments and BillPay API letting vertical SaaS platforms offer accounts-payable automation (including an AI card-conversion agent) and accounts-receivable automation to their own SMB customers.

disruptionMechanisms: Embedding AP/AR into vertical software instead of standalone tools
AI-driven ACH-to-card payment conversion to capture card interchange
attributes.businessModel: Embedded transaction/interchange revenue share plus platform fee
attributes.problemStatement: SMB software platforms want to own the AP/AR relationship with their customers but lack the infrastructure to build bill-pay and invoicing themselves.
attributes.productDescription: Mercoa provides an AI-driven bill-pay inbox, vendor onboarding, approval workflows, and a CardPay agent that converts ACH/check vendor payments to card where accepted, plus AI-assisted contract-to-invoice AR automation, delivered via React components, hosted iFrame, or REST API/SDKs.

founders.name: Sai Arora
founders.background: Listed as co-founder on Mercoa's Y Combinator (W23) company profile; further prior career detail not disclosed in sources reviewed.

founders.name: Sandeep Dinesh
founders.background: Previously Senior Software Engineer at Stripe, Lead Software Engineer at Furmacy (YC W21), and Senior Developer Advocate at Google Cloud, per Y Combinator's company profile.

name: Ansa
description: White-label stored-value wallet infrastructure ('wallet-as-a-service') letting merchants and platforms launch branded closed-loop balances, loyalty-linked funds, and incentive programs without building the ledger and compliance stack themselves.

disruptionMechanisms: Replacing card-network fees with closed-loop stored value
Enabling merchant-branded wallets without in-house build
attributes.goToMarket: Direct sales to merchants and platforms
attributes.problemStatement: Merchants want the customer-retention and fee benefits of stored-value programs (like Starbucks') but cannot justify building the ledger and compliance stack in-house.
attributes.productDescription: Ansa provides mobile SDKs and APIs for merchants and platforms to launch a white-labeled stored-value wallet, with a self-serve incentive engine and a merchant analytics portal tracking revenue, transaction frequency, and user segmentation.

founders.name: Sophia Goldberg
founders.background: Previously a product manager at Adyen, per TechCrunch.

founders.name: JT Cho
founders.background: Previously a software engineer at Affirm, per TechCrunch.

name: Kudos
description: AI-powered smart wallet (browser extension and app) that recommends the optimal credit card for a purchase and layers additional cash-back rewards across partner retailers.

disruptionMechanisms: Automating manual card-selection decisions at checkout
Aggregating card and cash-back data into one recommendation layer
attributes.problemStatement: Consumers routinely use the wrong card for a purchase and leave rewards value on the table because manually tracking optimal card-to-purchase matching is impractical.
attributes.productDescription: Kudos indexes 3,000-plus credit cards (MariaGPT), recommends the best card for a given purchase (Dream Wallet), and layers additional cash-back rewards across 15,000-plus partner retailers (Kudos Boost) via a browser extension and app.

founders.name: Tikue Anazodo
founders.background: Previously worked at Google and Affirm, per TechCrunch.

founders.name: Ahmad Ismail
founders.background: Previously worked at Google and Affirm alongside Anazodo, per TechCrunch.

name: Baselayer
description: AI-powered business identity verification (KYB), risk, and fraud platform for banks, credit unions, fintechs, marketplaces, and SMB lenders, combining public-records matching, a cross-customer fraud consortium, and portfolio monitoring.

disruptionMechanisms: Replacing manual KYB underwriting with real-time automated verification
Cross-customer fraud-consortium network effect
attributes.operationalModel: Pure software / API and data infrastructure
attributes.problemStatement: Verifying and monitoring the identity and risk of a business counterparty is still slow and manual, and legacy vendors have not kept pace with real-time fintech onboarding expectations.
attributes.productDescription: Baselayer matches applicant business data against Secretary of State filings across all 50 states, validates TINs with the IRS in real time, screens OFAC/sanctions, and layers liens, lawsuit/bankruptcy detection, and an AI 'Risk Co.Pilot,' backed by an Identity Network fraud consortium and continuous portfolio monitoring.

founders.name: Jonathan Awad
founders.background: Co-founded Baselayer in 2023 after encountering KYB gaps firsthand, per the company's May 2024 seed-round announcement.

founders.name: Timothy Hyde
founders.background: Named alongside Awad as co-founder in Baselayer's May 2024 seed-round announcement.

name: Felix Pago
description: WhatsApp-native remittance platform letting users send money to Latin America entirely inside a chat interface, using USDC stablecoin rails to reduce FX cost, with payout via bank deposit, mobile wallet, or cash pickup.

targetCustomers: Latino migrant workers in the United States sending money to Latin America
disruptionMechanisms: Removing app-download friction via a WhatsApp-native interface
Using stablecoin rails to reduce FX cost versus legacy remittance products
attributes.operationalModel: Pure software / consumer fintech with payout-network partnerships
attributes.problemStatement: Legacy remittance products require a dedicated app and charge high FX-inclusive fees, creating friction for underbanked, mobile-first senders who already live inside WhatsApp.
attributes.productDescription: Felix Pago lets users complete a cross-border money transfer, including by voice message, entirely inside WhatsApp, using Circle's USDC stablecoin to source and settle FX, with payout to bank accounts, mobile wallets, or 40,000-plus cash-pickup locations across ten Latin American countries.

founders.name: Manuel Godoy
founders.background: Identified as CEO in TechCrunch's May 2024 Series A coverage; further prior career detail not disclosed in sources reviewed.

founders.name: Bernardo García
founders.background: Identified alongside Godoy in TechCrunch's May 2024 Series A coverage; a third co-founder was referenced in that coverage but not individually named.

name: Formance
description: Open-source, programmable core ledger for fintech and regulated financial companies, with modules for ledger, connectivity, fund-lifecycle automation, and reconciliation, available self-hosted or as managed cloud.

disruptionMechanisms: Open-source distribution replacing costly in-house ledger builds
Hosted control plane monetizing free/self-hosted adoption
attributes.businessModel: Open-core: open-source free tier plus managed/hosted subscription
attributes.problemStatement: Fintechs need a reconciled, multi-rail ledger to track money movement across banks and partners, and most build one in-house at high cost and risk.
attributes.productDescription: Formance's Ledger module models transactions and balances using Numscript; Connectivity ingests data across banks, rails, wallets, and exchanges; Flows automates fund-lifecycle steps (holds, KYC, payouts); and Reconciliation matches internal ledger records against external statements, deployable self-hosted or via managed cloud.

founders.name: Clément Salaün
founders.background: Previously payments lead at Selency, per TechCrunch.

founders.name: Anne-Sybille Pradelles
founders.background: Previously COO of cybersecurity startup Alsid, per TechCrunch.

name: Keep
description: All-in-one financial platform for Canadian small and mid-size businesses combining a corporate credit card, expense automation, multi-currency banking, international payments, and growth-capital financing. Swapped into this batch in place of Moment, which had raised a Series C and outgrown the fund's check-size mandate.

technicalApproaches: Card issuing via bank partner (Peoples Trust / Mastercard)
disruptionMechanisms: Vertically integrating card, banking, and expense management for a market underserved by U.S.-first platforms
Higher credit limits via alternative underwriting
attributes.businessModel: Interchange plus subscription plus credit-facility spread
attributes.operationalModel: Pure software with regulated banking-partner dependency
attributes.problemStatement: Canadian SMBs are underserved by vertically integrated card-and-spend platforms because U.S. products (Brex, Ramp) do not natively fit Canadian banking and card economics.
attributes.productDescription: Keep provides a corporate Mastercard-branded card (issued by Peoples Trust) with credit limits 10-20x higher than typical small-business cards, expense automation with AI-powered categorization, multi-currency banking, international payments, and growth-capital financing up to $1m, targeting Canadian SMBs.

founders.name: Oliver Takach
founders.background: Two-time Y Combinator founder who previously bootstrapped a business to C$2m revenue by 2019, per the company's May 2025 funding announcement.

name: Layer
description: Embedded accounting infrastructure letting SMB software platforms offer accounting software and white-labeled, human-backed bookkeeping directly inside their own product via API and pre-built UI components.

disruptionMechanisms: Embedding accounting/bookkeeping into SMB software instead of a standalone tool
Owning the financial-home relationship for platform end customers
attributes.businessModel: Embedded subscription/service fee (software plus human bookkeeping)
attributes.problemStatement: SMB software platforms want to own the financial relationship with their customers but lack the infrastructure to build accounting and bookkeeping themselves.
attributes.productDescription: Layer offers embedded accounting software connected to a platform's transaction data, plus white-labeled full-service bookkeeping with dedicated human bookkeepers for monthly reconciliation and reporting, integrated via API and JavaScript UI components.

founders.name: Justin Meretab
founders.background: Co-founded Layer in 2023; further prior career detail not disclosed in sources reviewed.

founders.name: Daniel O'Neel
founders.background: Co-founded Layer alongside Meretab in 2023; further prior career detail not disclosed in sources reviewed.
